title: ophrys × kreutziana
intro: Ophrys × kreutziana, also known as the Kreutz's bee orchid or bee orchid, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Ophrys sphegodes and Ophrys lusitanica. It is known to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Ophrys × kreutziana include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Ophrys × kreutziana are typically made by infusing dried flowers in hot water or ethanol, and can be used in traditional medicine for various purposes, although its use and efficacy require further scientific study.
